blanks.sentence: Adults purchasing children's toys for personal use make up between _____ of the total toy market
blanks.evidence: "adults buying kids' toys for themselves account for between 30 and 40% of the entire toy market"

blanks.sentence: Marketers have coined a new word for such consumers: _____
blanks.evidence: "advertisers have come up with a term for them: kidults – adults who are interested in toys and games intended for children"

blanks.sentence: In a single three-month period ending in December, American kidults spent _____ on toys for themselves
blanks.evidence: "American kidults spent over 1.5 billion dollars on toys for themselves"

blanks.sentence: Among the most sought-after items were _____, also known as plushies
blanks.evidence: "Among the most popular were plush toys, or plushies for short – toy dolls or animals made from cloth and filled with soft material"

blanks.sentence: These toys are constructed from cloth and packed with _____
blanks.evidence: "toy dolls or animals made from cloth and filled with soft material"

blanks.sentence: The growing trend is largely fuelled by _____ and social media
blanks.evidence: "the toy trend is being driven by online shoppers and social media"

blanks.sentence: Lisa runs a toy brand called _____, which specialises in leather plushies
blanks.evidence: "the owner of Leather Monsters, a brand of leather plushies which she advertises online"

blanks.sentence: Her business is headquartered in _____, Canada
blanks.evidence: "Lisa Lee is a toy maker based in Vancouver"

blanks.sentence: She promotes her products _____
blanks.evidence: "a brand of leather plushies which she advertises online"

blanks.sentence: Her renewed interest in toys began once she secured a _____
blanks.evidence: "Once I kind of had a stable job with disposable income, I kind of rediscovered my love for soft toys"

blanks.sentence: She uses her spare money — known as _____ — to fund her hobby
blanks.evidence: "Disposable income is the money you have left over after paying all your expenses"

blanks.sentence: Collecting toys helps her reconnect with her _____, a psychological concept referring to the childlike part of one's adult self
blanks.evidence: "help her reconnect with her inner child – a term from psychology which describes the part of your adult personality that still feels and reacts like a child"

blanks.sentence: Jay runs a YouTube channel under the name _____
blanks.evidence: "Jay Glatfelter is an enthusiastic toy collector and influencer who makes YouTube videos under the name Geek Dad Life"
blanks.section: Profile: Jay Glatfelter – Toy Collector & Influencer

blanks.sentence: During the pandemic, a large number of new collectors entered the hobby, causing secondary market prices to _____
blanks.evidence: "we saw the prices – the secondary market prices – like, explode once the pandemic happened"
blanks.section: Profile: Jay Glatfelter – Toy Collector & Influencer

blanks.sentence: Many people joined because they had _____ — meaning plenty of free time — during lockdowns
blanks.evidence: "people had time on their hands – an idiom meaning to have a lot of free time and nothing to do with it"
blanks.section: Profile: Jay Glatfelter – Toy Collector & Influencer

blanks.sentence: Others had extra funds as they were no longer _____ on going out
blanks.evidence: "maybe they had extra money because they're not spending it by going out"
blanks.section: Profile: Jay Glatfelter – Toy Collector & Influencer

blanks.sentence: The cost of sought-after items such as Pokémon cards and Jellycat plushies rose _____ throughout the pandemic
blanks.evidence: "the cost of collectable items, like Pokémon cards and Jellycat plushies, increasing dramatically during the pandemic"
blanks.section: Profile: Jay Glatfelter – Toy Collector & Influencer

blanks.sentence: Kidult: a grown-up who enjoys _____ meant for younger audiences
blanks.evidence: "kidult – an adult who likes playing with toys and games intended for children"

blanks.sentence: Plushie: a soft toy stuffed with _____
blanks.evidence: "a toy doll or animal stuffed with a soft cloth filling"

blanks.sentence: To have time on your hands: to possess _____ with little to occupy oneself
blanks.evidence: "having free time and nothing much to do with it"

multipleChoice.question: What does Beth suggest about the current trend of adults purchasing toys?
options: It is a passing fad driven mainly by nostalgia and social pressure
It is more widespread than most people would initially expect
It is largely confined to collectors with specialist knowledge
It is a phenomenon that advertisers have been slow to recognise
multipleChoice.evidence: Beth: "grown-ups playing with toys is more common than you might think"

multipleChoice.question: According to the information given, which of the following best describes what "kidults" refers to?
options: Children who prefer adult-themed entertainment over traditional play
Marketers who design products targeting both children and grown-ups
Retailers who stock both children's and adult merchandise in one store
Grown-ups who have a genuine interest in products designed for younger audiences
multipleChoice.evidence: Beth: "kidults – adults who are interested in toys and games intended for children"

multipleChoice.question: What does Lisa Lee suggest was the primary condition that allowed her to resume buying toys as an adult?
options: Having financial stability and money to spend beyond her basic needs
Gaining recognition through her online channel and social media presence
Reconnecting with childhood friends who shared her interest in collecting
Receiving encouragement from her employer to pursue personal hobbies
multipleChoice.evidence: Lisa Lee: "Once I kind of had a stable job with disposable income, I kind of rediscovered my love for soft toys"

multipleChoice.question: According to Lisa Lee, what psychological function do toys serve for her beyond simple enjoyment?
options: They help her manage professional stress and workplace anxiety effectively
They give her a creative outlet for designing and crafting new products
They allow her to nurture and restore a childlike aspect of her adult self
They serve as financial investments that grow in value over time
multipleChoice.evidence: Lisa: "reconnecting with my childhood, and healing that inner child, and I think the toys also play a part in that"

multipleChoice.question: What does Jay Glatfelter say happened to prices in the secondary toy market after the pandemic began?
options: They gradually stabilised as supply chains recovered and demand levelled off
They surged dramatically as a large wave of new collectors entered the market
They dropped initially before recovering as online platforms expanded their reach
They fluctuated unpredictably due to shortages in manufacturing and distribution
multipleChoice.evidence: Jay: "we saw the prices – the secondary market prices – like, explode once the pandemic happened, because you had people that had time on their hands"

multipleChoice.question: According to Jay Glatfelter, which combination of factors contributed to the rise in adult toy collecting during the pandemic?
options: Increased advertising by toy brands and a growing interest in retro culture
Government stimulus payments and the closure of major toy retail chains
Reduced working hours and a widespread desire to support local businesses
An abundance of free time alongside money that was no longer spent on outings
multipleChoice.evidence: Jay: "you had people that had time on their hands, maybe they had extra money because they're not spending it by going out, and now they're putting it into collecting toys"

multipleChoice.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between adult nostalgia and the cost of collectables during the pandemic?
options: Nostalgia had little measurable impact on the pricing of collectables overall
Collectors deliberately avoided high-priced items in favour of affordable alternatives
The emotional desire to recapture childhood memories contributed to rising prices
Manufacturers lowered prices strategically to attract nostalgic adult consumers
multipleChoice.evidence: Phil: "the cost of collectable items, like Pokémon cards and Jellycat plushies, increasing dramatically during the pandemic"

multipleChoice.question: What is Phil's implied attitude when he describes the feeling of rediscovering childhood as something that "comes at a price"?
options: He acknowledges that emotional fulfilment through collecting involves a financial cost
He argues that toy collecting is an irresponsible use of disposable income for adults
He suggests that childhood memories are too precious to be assigned a monetary value
He warns that the toy industry is deliberately exploiting consumers' sentimental feelings
multipleChoice.evidence: Phil: "the feeling of rediscovering your childhood… comes at a price, with the cost of collectable items… increasing dramatically"

multipleChoice.question: What does the example of the Lego Millennium Falcon most strongly suggest about the premium toy market?
options: Licensed products based on classic films consistently outsell all other toy categories
Lego sets are generally considered poor value compared to other collectable items
The Harry Potter franchise generates more consumer interest than the Star Wars brand
Certain collectable toys can command remarkably high prices in mainstream retail
multipleChoice.evidence: Phil mentioned the Lego Star Wars Millennium Falcon with a UK retail price of £734.99

multipleChoice.question: Based on the overall discussion, what broader conclusion can be drawn about the adult toy market?
options: It is primarily sustained by a small group of wealthy enthusiasts with niche interests
It represents a significant and commercially influential segment of consumer spending
It is expected to decline once the social effects of the pandemic fully subside
It depends almost entirely on social media trends rather than genuine personal interest
multipleChoice.evidence: Beth: "adults buying children's toys account for between 30 and 40% of the entire toy market"; Phil: "American kidults spent over 1.5 billion dollars on toys for themselves"
